text.title: Explicit refusal field at top of strict schema
text.summary: Declare nullable refusal first so safety declines parse cleanly into a typed branch instead of breaking strict-mode JSON.

section.title: Mirror the OpenAI refusal contract
section.p: OpenAI's strict-mode response already includes a top-level `refusal` string when the model declines. Mirroring it inside your own schema gives the same contract on Anthropic and local backends and turns refusal handling from exception-driven into typed-branch code.

rule.statement: For any strict-mode SO schema in a safety-sensitive domain, add `refusal: str | None = None` as the FIRST field; mark every payload field nullable; check `response.refusal` in a single branch before reading the payload.
rule.rationale: Strict mode forbids missing required fields; without a refusal channel the model must either invent fake values or violate the grammar. The explicit field gives the model a legal path to decline.

example.note: Single typed branch handles refusal; payload fields stay null-safe.

example.code:
class MedicalExtract(BaseModel): diagnoses: list[str] # required, no refusal channel medications: list[str]
# model invents [] or violates grammar → exception
example.note: Model has no legal way to refuse; you get fake empty lists or parser errors.

antipattern.description: Detecting refusals by string-matching the raw response text for "I cannot" / "Sorry".
antipattern.reason: Locale- and model-dependent, breaks on every model upgrade, indistinguishable from legitimate content containing those words.

section.reference: Pydantic schema with refusal-first, nullable payload pattern.
OpenAI Structured Outputs launch — native refusal field.
OpenAI SO guide — refusal handling section.
